name: workflows
context: Define GitHub Actions workflow YAML — triggers, jobs, steps, matrix strategies, and reusable workflows
info: Define CI/CD workflows in `.github/workflows/*.yml` using GitHub Actions workflow syntax. Covers trigger events (push, pull_request, schedule, workflow_dispatch), job configuration (runs-on, steps, needs), matrix strategies for multi-environment testing, reusable workflows for DRY configuration, and composite actions. Workflows integrate with [[github/pulls]] via pull_request triggers and required status checks. See [[github/actions/runs]] for monitoring execution and [[github/actions/artifacts]] for build output management. See [[github/auth]] for token management within workflows.
body:
# Workflow Definition

Workflows are YAML files in `.github/workflows/` that define automated pipelines.

## Basic Workflow

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml
name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm test

  lint:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
          cache: npm
      - run: npm ci
      - run: npm run lint
```

## Trigger Events

```yaml
on:
  # Push to specific branches
  push:
    branches: [main, develop]
    paths: ["src/**", "package.json"]
    tags: ["v*"]

  # Pull request events
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]
    branches: [main]

  # Scheduled (cron)
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 6 * * 1"  # Every Monday at 6 AM UTC

  # Manual trigger
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      environment:
        description: "Deployment environment"
        required: true
        type: choice
        options: [staging, production]
      version:
        description: "Version to deploy"
        required: true
        type: string

  # Triggered by another workflow
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      node-version:
        type: string
        default: "20"
    secrets:
      NPM_TOKEN:
        required: true

  # Issue and comment events
  issues:
    types: [opened, labeled]
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

  # Release events
  release:
    types: [published]
```

## Matrix Strategy

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        node-version: [18, 20, 22]
        exclude:
          - os: windows-latest
            node-version: 18
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            node-version: 20
            experimental: true
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - run: npm ci && npm test
```

## Job Dependencies

```yaml
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci && npm test

  build:
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - run: npm ci && npm run build

  deploy:
    needs: [test, build]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
    environment: production
    steps:
      - run: echo "Deploying..."
```

## Reusable Workflows

```yaml
# .github/workflows/reusable-test.yml (called workflow)
name: Reusable Test
on:
  workflow_call:
    inputs:
      node-version:
        type: string
        default: "20"
jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ inputs.node-version }}
      - run: npm ci && npm test
```

```yaml
# .github/workflows/ci.yml (caller workflow)
name: CI
on: [push, pull_request]
jobs:
  test:
    uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable-test.yml
    with:
      node-version: "20"
    secrets: inherit
```

## Secrets and Environment Variables

```yaml
jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      NODE_ENV: production
    steps:
      - run: echo "Token is ${{ secrets.DEPLOY_TOKEN }}"
      - run: echo "Node env is $NODE_ENV"
      - env:
          STEP_VAR: "step-level"
        run: echo "$STEP_VAR"
```

## Permissions

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  issues: write

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
```

sections.title: Trigger events
sections.body: Workflows trigger on `push`, `pull_request`, `schedule` (cron), `workflow_dispatch` (manual), `workflow_call` (reusable), `issues`, `release`, and 30+ other events. Filter with `branches`, `paths`, `tags`, and `types`. Use `workflow_dispatch.inputs` for parameterized manual triggers. See [[github/webhooks]] for the full event catalog.

sections.title: Matrix strategies
sections.body: Use `strategy.matrix` to run jobs across multiple configurations. GitHub creates a job instance for each combination. Use `fail-fast: false` to continue when one combination fails. `exclude` removes specific combinations. `include` adds extra combinations or variables to existing ones. Maximum matrix size is 256 jobs.

sections.title: Reusable workflows
sections.body: Define reusable workflows with `on: workflow_call` that other workflows invoke with `uses:`. Accepts `inputs` and `secrets`. Call with `uses: ./.github/workflows/reusable.yml` (same repo) or `uses: owner/repo/.github/workflows/reusable.yml@main` (cross-repo). Use `secrets: inherit` to pass all secrets from the caller.

sections.title: Permissions and GITHUB_TOKEN
sections.body: Every workflow gets an automatic `GITHUB_TOKEN` with configurable permissions. Set `permissions:` at workflow or job level to restrict scope. Default permissions can be configured at repo or org level. Use `contents: read` and `pull-requests: write` for typical CI workflows. See [[github/auth]] for external token management.

related.note: This is a sub-skill of the actions skill
After defining workflows, monitor their runs
PR events are the most common workflow trigger
